# AI MODEL SELECTION: Privacy & Threat Analysis (March 2026 Update)

**Date:** March 6, 2026
**Supersedes:** December 8, 2025 edition
**Context:** Common Cloud deployment for vulnerable communities (NSL/subpoena threat model)

---

## WHAT CHANGED SINCE DECEMBER 2025

Since the December 2025 edition of this document:

- **New models:** Claude Opus 4.6 & Sonnet 4.6 (Feb 2026), OpenAI GPT-5.4 (Mar 5, 2026), Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview (Feb 2026), Llama 4 family (Spring 2025), Mistral 3 family (Dec 2025)
- **Major court ruling:** US v. Heppner (Feb 2026) — AI chats have **no attorney-client privilege**
- **NYT v. OpenAI escalation:** Judge ordered OpenAI to turn over **20 million ChatGPT logs** (Jan 2026)
- **Anthropic $1.5B settlement:** Bartz v. Anthropic class action over pirated training data (Aug 2025)
- **DeepSeek regulatory storm:** Banned in Italy and South Korea; banned on US federal and multiple state government devices; third-party analysis found data sharing with China Mobile
- **OpenAI-Pentagon deal:** OpenAI contracted for classified Pentagon AI networks (Feb-Mar 2026); Anthropic was blacklisted for refusing to allow weapons/surveillance use
- **GDPR enforcement:** Italy fined OpenAI €15M (Dec 2024), fined Replika €5M (May 2025), banned DeepSeek (Jan 2025)
- **Privacy policy updates:** Anthropic (Jan 2026), OpenAI DPA (Jan 2026); Anthropic 5-year retention still in effect for opted-in consumers
- **New state AI laws:** California SB 53 and AB 2013, Texas RAIGA — all effective Jan 1, 2026

### Current Model Lineup (March 2026)

| Provider | Flagship Model | Released |
|----------|---------------|----------|
| Anthropic | **Claude Opus 4.6** | Feb 5, 2026 |
| Anthropic | **Claude Sonnet 4.6** | Feb 17, 2026 |
| Anthropic | **Claude Haiku 4.5** | Oct 1, 2025 |
| OpenAI | **GPT-5.4** | Mar 5, 2026 |
| OpenAI | **o3-pro / o4-mini** | 2025 |
| Google | **Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview** | Feb 2026 |
| Google | **Gemini 3 Flash Preview** | Jan 2026 |
| DeepSeek | **DeepSeek V3.1** | Aug 21, 2025 |
| Meta | **Llama 4 Scout / Maverick / Behemoth** | Spring 2025 |
| Mistral | **Mistral Large 3 / Ministral 3** | Dec 2025 |

---

## PART 1: MARCH 2026 MODEL LANDSCAPE

### **OpenAI GPT-5.4 / GPT-5.2 / o3-pro**

**Release Dates:**
- GPT-5.4: March 5, 2026 (latest flagship)
- GPT-5.2: December 2025
- GPT-5.1: November 2025
- o3-pro / o4-mini: 2025 (reasoning models)

**Key Features (GPT-5.4):**
- **1M context window**
- **Native computer-use:** First OpenAI model with built-in computer interaction
- **Pro tier:** Higher-quality output at premium pricing
- **GPT-5-mini and GPT-5-nano** available for budget deployments

**API Pricing (per 1M tokens):**

| Model | Input | Output |
|-------|-------|--------|
| GPT-5.4 | $2.50 | $15.00 |
| GPT-5.4 Pro | $30.00 | $180.00 |
| GPT-5.2 | $1.75 | $14.00 |
| GPT-5.1 | $1.25 | $10.00 |
| GPT-5-mini | $0.25 | $2.00 |
| GPT-5-nano | $0.05 | $0.40 |
| o3-pro | $20.00 | $80.00 |
| o3 | $2.00 | $8.00 |
| o4-mini | $1.10 | $4.40 |

**Consumer Plans:**
- ChatGPT Plus: $20/mo
- ChatGPT Pro: $200/mo (includes GPT-5.4 Pro)

---

### **Claude Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6 / Haiku 4.5**

**Release Dates:**
- Opus 4.6: February 5, 2026
- Sonnet 4.6: February 17, 2026
- Haiku 4.5: October 1, 2025 (no Haiku 4.6 yet)
- Legacy: Opus 4.5 (Nov 2025), Sonnet 4.5 (Sept 2025) still available

**Key Features:**
- **Opus 4.6:** Most intelligent model; best for agents and complex coding. 200K context (1M beta). Extended thinking + adaptive thinking.
- **Sonnet 4.6:** Everyday workhorse; reportedly outperforms Opus on some real-world office tasks. 200K context (1M beta).
- **Haiku 4.5:** Fastest, lowest cost. 200K context.
- **Claude 3 Haiku:** Deprecated, retiring April 19, 2026.

**API Pricing (per 1M tokens):**

| Model | Input | Output |
|-------|-------|--------|
| Claude Opus 4.6 | $5.00 | $25.00 |
| Claude Sonnet 4.6 | $3.00 | $15.00 |
| Claude Haiku 4.5 | $1.00 | $5.00 |

**Availability:**
- Claude.ai (Pro, Max, Team Premium users)
- Claude Code (Pro users)
- API (anthropic.com)
- Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI

---

### **Google Gemini 3.1 Pro / Gemini 3 Flash**

**Release Dates:**
- Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview: February 2026
- Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview: February 2026
- Gemini 3 Flash Preview: January 2026
- Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash: Stable (2025)

**Key Features (Gemini 3.1 Pro):**
- **77.1% ARC-AGI-2 score**
- **Native video understanding**
- **Agentic capabilities**
- **1M context window**

**API Pricing (per 1M tokens):**

| Model | Input | Output |
|-------|-------|--------|
| Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview | $2.00 (≤200K) / $4.00 | $12.00 / $18.00 |
| Gemini 3 Flash Preview | $0.50 | $3.00 |
| Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite | $0.25 | $1.50 |
| Gemini 2.5 Pro | $1.25 / $2.50 | $10.00 / $15.00 |
| Gemini 2.5 Flash | $0.30 | $2.50 |

**Consumer Plans:**
- Free tier available through Google AI Studio
- Pro: $19.99/mo
- Ultra: $124.99/3 months

---

### **DeepSeek V3.1 / V3 / R1**

**Status (March 2026):**
- **DeepSeek V3.1:** Released August 21, 2025. MIT License. Hybrid thinking/non-thinking modes. +40% over V3 on SWE-bench.
- **DeepSeek V3 (V3-0324):** 671B MoE, still available.
- **DeepSeek R1:** Dedicated reasoning model (OpenAI o1-level).
- **DeepSeek V4:** NOT YET RELEASED. Originally targeted mid-Feb 2026, delayed. Expected ~1 trillion params.
- **DeepSeek R2:** NOT YET RELEASED. Delayed due to data labeling and chip constraints.

**API Pricing:** ~$0.55/M input tokens (dramatically cheaper than Western competitors).

**Self-Hosted:** Open-source weights available on HuggingFace.

---

### **Meta Llama 4 (Open-Weight)**

**Released:** Spring 2025

| Model | Active Params | Experts | Key Feature |
|-------|--------------|---------|-------------|
| Llama 4 Scout | 17B | 16 (MoE) | 10M context window. Fits single H100. |
| Llama 4 Maverick | 17B | 128 (MoE) | Beats GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 Flash on benchmarks. |
| Llama 4 Behemoth | 288B | 16 (MoE) | Outperforms GPT-4.5, Claude Sonnet 3.7, Gemini 2.0 Pro on STEM. |

- Natively multimodal (not bolted-on vision)
- Free and open-weight
- Mixture-of-Experts architecture

---

### **Mistral 3 (Open-Source, Apache 2.0)**

**Released:** December 2025

| Model | Parameters | Notes |
|-------|-----------|-------|
| Mistral Large 3 | 41B active / 675B MoE | Flagship. 256K context. Apache 2.0. |
| Ministral 3 14B | 14B dense | Base, instruct, reasoning variants. |
| Ministral 3 8B | 8B dense | Same variant options. |
| Ministral 3 3B | 3B dense | Edge-friendly. |
| Devstral 2 / Small 2 | 24B | Coding-focused. |

---

## PART 2: PRIVACY & THREAT ANALYSIS BY PROVIDER

### **CRITICAL FINDING: AI CHAT HISTORY HAS NO LEGAL PRIVILEGE**

**Key Court Rulings (2025-2026):**

1. **US v. Heppner (S.D.N.Y., Feb 10, 2026):** Judge Jed Rakoff ruled that conversations between a criminal defendant and Anthropic's Claude are **NOT protected** by attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine. Reasoning: Claude is not a lawyer, and "discussion of legal issues between two non-attorneys is not protected." The defendant had no reasonable expectation of confidentiality. This is the first federal ruling directly addressing AI chat privilege.

2. **NYT v. OpenAI (S.D.N.Y., Jan 2026):** Judge Sidney Stein upheld an order compelling OpenAI to produce its **entire 20-million-log sample** of ChatGPT conversations. OpenAI's attempt to hand over only cherry-picked logs was rejected. Only Enterprise, Education, and ZDR customers are excluded from the preservation order.

3. **FBI Seizure of CEO's AI Chats (2026):** FBI seized a CEO's computer and sought access to his AI chat history. Court left only a narrow, untested exception: if an attorney specifically directed the client to conduct AI research as part of attorney work product.

4. **Criminal Prosecutions (2025):** Three criminal cases cited ChatGPT conversations as evidence — involving child exploitation, arson, and vandalism.

5. **Divorce/Custody Cases (2024-2026):** Multiple cases used ChatGPT/Claude history as evidence. California courts are now implementing AI document review for family law (mandatory by July 2025 for courts serving 500K+ populations).

**Legal Precedent Established:**
- AI chat history is treated like any digital evidence (texts, emails)
- Admissible in court
- **No attorney-client privilege** (US v. Heppner)
- Deletion after preservation notice = spoliation (sanctions)
- Half of AI users are unaware their chats can be compelled as evidence (survey, 2025)
- Sam Altman publicly warned that people treating ChatGPT like a therapist/lawyer should understand those conversations can be subpoenaed

---

### **OpenAI Transparency: Government Requests (H1 2025)**

OpenAI's published government data request report for January-June 2025:
- **119 requests** for user account information
- **26 requests** for chat content
- **1 emergency request**
- Policy: requires subpoena for account info, valid warrant for content

**OpenAI also operates a monitoring system** that scans all ChatGPT conversations for potentially harmful content, escalating concerning conversations to human reviewers who can report to law enforcement.

---

### **OpenAI (ChatGPT / GPT-5.4) Privacy Analysis**

#### **Data Retention Policy (Updated Jan 2026)**

**ChatGPT Consumer (Plus/Pro, $20-200/mo):**
- **Default:** Chats retained indefinitely for model improvement unless chat history is disabled
- **History off:** Retained 30 days for abuse monitoring, then deleted; not used for training
- **BUT:** NYT litigation hold requires preservation regardless of user settings. Only Enterprise, Education, and ZDR customers are excluded.

**ChatGPT API / Business:**
- **Default API:** 30-day retention for safety monitoring, then auto-deleted
- **API data not used for training by default** (since March 2023)
- **Zero Data Retention (ZDR):** Available for qualifying enterprise customers — no storage of prompts or responses, no human review. Requires prior OpenAI approval. Not all endpoints qualify.
- **Enterprise/Team:** Data never used for training

**Government Data Request Policy (effective Jan 1, 2026):** New published policy formalizing law enforcement request procedures.

#### **Training Data Opt-Out**

**How to Opt Out:**
1. Settings → Data Controls → "Improve the model for everyone" (toggle off)
2. OR: Enable "Temporary Chat" mode
3. API: Data not used for training by default

**Effect:**
- ✅ Your chats won't train future models
- ⚠️ Still retained 30 days (abuse monitoring)
- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides — NYT litigation hold)

---

#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**

| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
|----------|-----------|-----|
| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | FBI can serve NSL directly to OpenAI; gag order prevents disclosure; monitoring system already scans all chats |
| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Texas/AZ AG can subpoena OpenAI (US company, US jurisdiction); immediate compliance likely |
| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | OpenAI is US-based; fined €15M by Italy (Dec 2024); cross-border transfer concerns |
| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Multiple precedents; courts routinely subpoena ChatGPT history |
| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer can request logs if using company API keys; individual use can be subpoenaed |
| **Pentagon/Military** | 🔴 **NEW RISK** | OpenAI contracted for classified Pentagon networks (Feb-Mar 2026); contractual "red lines" on mass surveillance criticized as insufficient by EFF |

**Key Risk:** OpenAI is **US-based (San Francisco)**, subject to US courts, Five Eyes intelligence sharing, and NSL/FISA court orders. New Pentagon contract creates additional government entanglement.

---

### **Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6) Privacy Analysis**

#### **Data Retention Policy (Updated Jan 12, 2026)**

**MAJOR POLICY (Sept 2025, still in effect):**
- **Previous:** 30-day retention (all users)
- **Current:** **5-year retention** of de-identified data if you opt in to model training

**Consumer Accounts (Free, Pro, Max):**

| Setting | Retention | Training Use |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| "Help improve Claude" = **ON** | 5 years (de-identified) | Yes |
| "Help improve Claude" = **OFF** | 30 days then deleted | No |
| Trust & safety flagged content | Up to 2 years (inputs/outputs) | N/A |
| Trust & safety classification scores | Up to 7 years | N/A |
| User feedback submissions | 5 years | N/A |

**Key clarification:** 5-year retention only covers **new or resumed chats** after the setting is enabled — not retroactive conversations.

**Business/Enterprise (Claude for Work, API, Bedrock, Vertex):**
- Anthropic acts as **data processor** under separate Data Processing Agreements
- Data is **NOT used for training**
- Consumer privacy policy does not apply
- Includes: Claude for Work, Claude for Education, Claude Gov

**Jan 2026 Update:** Added Consumer Health Data Privacy Policy for US users integrating third-party health apps; consolidated regional supplemental disclosures.

#### **GDPR Entity:**
- **EEA/UK/Switzerland:** Anthropic Ireland, Limited
- **All other regions:** Anthropic PBC (San Francisco, CA)
- Cross-border transfers via adequacy decisions and Standard Contractual Clauses

#### **Training Data Opt-Out**

**How to Opt Out:**
1. Settings → Privacy Settings → "Help improve Claude" (toggle OFF)
2. File Data Processing Objection (optional, for formal record)

**Effect:**
- ✅ Data not used for training
- ✅ Retention reduced to 30 days
- ❌ Lose personalization/memory features
- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)
- ❌ Trust & safety exceptions still apply (2-7 year retention for flagged content)

#### **Anthropic Legal Exposure (New Since Dec 2025)**

- **Bartz v. Anthropic ($1.5B settlement, Aug 2025):** Anthropic settled a class action over using pirated books (from shadow libraries) to train Claude. ~$3,000 per title for ~500,000 books. Claims deadline: March 30, 2026. Final approval hearing: April 2026.
- **Reddit v. Anthropic (Feb 2026):** Reddit sued Anthropic for deploying ~24,000 automated bots to scrape Reddit content over 100,000 times. Claims breach of contract, unjust enrichment, trespass to chattels.
- **Music Publishers v. Anthropic (M.D. Tenn., ongoing):** UMG, Concord, and others sued for massive-scale music lyric copyright infringement via web scraping.
- **Anthropic refused Pentagon contract (Feb 2026):** Blacklisted by Trump administration for refusing to allow AI use for autonomous weapons or mass domestic surveillance. OpenAI took the contract instead.

---

#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**

| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
|----------|-----------|-----|
| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Anthropic is US-based (SF); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; 5-year retention if opted in = extended exposure |
| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction, immediate compliance likely |
| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | US-based company with Irish subsidiary; GDPR deletion rights may conflict with 5-year retention |
| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US v. Heppner (Feb 2026) explicitly addressed Claude conversations — no privilege |
| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer API keys = employer can request logs; individual use can be subpoenaed |

**Key Risk:** Anthropic is **US-based (San Francisco)**, subject to same legal framework as OpenAI. US v. Heppner specifically established that Claude conversations are discoverable.

---

### **Google (Gemini 3.1 Pro) Privacy Analysis**

#### **Data Retention Policy (March 2026)**

**Gemini Consumer App:**
- **Default:** Prompts and responses stored up to 30 days for debugging and abuse detection
- **Opt-out:** Settings → Gemini Apps Activity → toggle OFF
- **If disabled:** Conversations not saved, not used for training; metadata still logged

**Vertex AI (Business):**
- **In-memory cache:** 24-hour TTL, project-level isolation
- **Zero Data Retention:** Must be explicitly enabled **per API call** by setting a specific field in the request
- **Session Resumption must NOT be enabled** to achieve true ZDR
- SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA available

**Google Workspace:**
- Google states it will **not use customer data to train or fine-tune AI/ML models** without prior permission
- Admins can shorten or disable prompt storage entirely

**EU Data Residency:**
- Configurable data regions: **US**, **EU**, or **No preference**
- Vertex AI Platform supports configurable data location
- Supported EU regions: europe-west1 (Belgium), europe-west3 (Frankfurt), etc.

**Class Action (Oct 2025):** Lawsuit alleges Google activated Gemini AI features across Gmail, Chat, and Meet **without user consent**, enabling AI to read private communications. Putative class of all U.S. Google account holders.

**$425.7M Verdict (Dec 2025):** Federal jury awarded $425.7M to ~98M Google users misled about data collection opt-outs on mobile devices.

#### **Training Data Opt-Out**

**Gemini App:**
1. Gemini Apps Activity settings → Toggle OFF "Save Gemini Apps Activity"
2. Delete existing history (optional)

**Workspace:** Admin console → Settings → Disable AI training

**Effect:**
- ✅ Conversations not used for training
- ⚠️ Metadata still logged (abuse/compliance)
- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)

---

#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**

| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
|----------|-----------|-----|
| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Google is US-based (CA); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; extensive data infrastructure |
| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction; Google has cooperated with law enforcement historically |
| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **MODERATE** | Google has EU data centers and configurable data residency; better than OpenAI/Anthropic but still US parent company |
| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Same risk as ChatGPT/Claude |
| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Workspace admins can access user logs; class action alleging non-consensual Gemini activation pending |

**Advantage over OpenAI/Anthropic:** Google has native EU data residency options, configurable per Vertex AI project. Best GDPR posture of the three major US providers.

---

### **DeepSeek (V3.1) Privacy Analysis**

#### **Data Retention Policy (March 2026)**

**DeepSeek API:**
- **China-based:** Hangzhou DeepSeek Artificial Intelligence Co., Ltd.
- **All data stored in the People's Republic of China** (explicitly stated in privacy policy)
- **No specific retention periods disclosed:** Policy says "as long as necessary to provide our Services" — vague and non-compliant with GDPR transparency requirements
- **Data collected:** Account info, user inputs (text, voice, prompts, uploaded files, chat history), device/network info (IP, device model, OS, device ID), approximate location, cookies, payment data
- **Training use:** Personal data used "to improve and develop the Services and to train and improve our technology" — opt-out mechanism unclear
- **Third-party analysis found:** DeepSeek app can capture login information and share it with China Mobile (state-owned entity)
- **GDPR representative:** Prighter Group appointed as Art. 27 representative, but DeepSeek has claimed GDPR does not apply to it

**Self-Hosted (Open-Source):**
- You control all data — run on your own servers
- No external transmission
- Zero retention risk

#### **Regulatory Actions Against DeepSeek**

| Country/Region | Action | Status |
|----------------|--------|--------|
| **Italy** | Garante banned DeepSeek R1 from app stores (Jan 2025) | **Banned** — app removed |
| **South Korea** | PIPC ordered removal from stores; defense ministry banned on military computers (Feb 2025) | **Banned** — under investigation |
| **US Federal** | "No DeepSeek on Government Devices Act" (HR 1121) introduced Feb 2025; Navy and Commerce Dept banned on govt devices; 21 state AGs urged passage | **Pending legislation** |
| **Texas** | First state to ban DeepSeek on all state devices (Jan 2025) | **Banned** |
| **New York, Tennessee, Virginia** | Followed Texas with state device bans | **Banned** |
| **Belgium, France, Ireland** | Regulatory inquiries into data processing | **Under investigation** |
| **Germany (Berlin)** | Asked DeepSeek to voluntarily remove apps; sent notice-and-action to Apple/Google | **Under pressure** |

#### **Distillation Attacks (Feb 2026)**

Anthropic publicly accused DeepSeek and two other Chinese AI labs (Moonshot, MiniMax) of **industrial-scale distillation attacks** — using ~24,000 fraudulent accounts to generate 16 million+ exchanges with Claude to extract capabilities. DeepSeek specifically used Claude to generate censorship-safe alternatives for politically sensitive queries.

---

#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**

| Scenario | Risk Level | Explanation |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | ⚠️ **COMPLICATED** | China-based; US courts have no jurisdiction; BUT Chinese gov't can access data under National Security Law |
| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🟢 **LOW** | US courts cannot compel Chinese company |
| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | ⚠️ **COMPLICATED** | Banned in Italy; GDPR extraterritorial scope applies but enforcement is difficult |
| **Chinese Gov't Access** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Chinese National Security Law requires companies to provide data on request (no judicial oversight); data confirmed stored in China |

**Recommendation for Vulnerable Communities:** **DO NOT use DeepSeek API** if serving populations at risk from Chinese government (Uyghurs, Hong Kong activists, Taiwan-related orgs) OR from data sharing with US adversaries (given pending federal legislation).

**Safe Alternative:** **Self-host DeepSeek V3.1** (MIT License, open-source weights) on your own servers → zero external data transmission.

---

## PART 3: COURT CASES & REGULATORY LANDSCAPE

### **Active Litigation (March 2026)**

#### Copyright / Training Data Cases

| Case | Court | Status | Key Ruling/Implication |
|------|-------|--------|----------------------|
| **NYT v. OpenAI** | S.D.N.Y. | Discovery phase; no trial date | Jan 2026: 20M chat logs ordered produced. Mar 2026: OpenAI seeking NYT's ChatGPT prompts. No final fair use ruling yet. |
| **Bartz v. Anthropic** | N.D. Cal. | $1.5B settlement pending | Training on legally acquired works = fair use; pirated books = NOT fair use. Claims deadline Mar 30, 2026. |
| **Kadrey v. Meta** | N.D. Cal. | Decided (June 2025) | Meta's LLM training was "highly transformative and fair use." |
| **Thomson Reuters v. ROSS Intelligence** | 3rd Circuit (appeal) | On appeal | Trial court found training was NOT fair use. Key appellate decision pending. |
| **Andersen v. Stability AI, Midjourney** | N.D. Cal. | Active discovery | Core copyright claims survived dismissal (Aug 2024). |
| **Disney/Universal/Warner Bros. v. Midjourney** | TBD | Filed June/Nov 2025 | Alleging reproduction of Marvel/Star Wars characters. |
| **Reddit v. Anthropic** | CA state | Filed Feb 2026 | ~24K fake accounts, 100K+ scraping events alleged. |
| **Music Publishers v. Anthropic** | M.D. Tenn. | Ongoing | Massive-scale lyric copyright infringement. |

**Overall:** 51+ copyright lawsuits against AI companies as of Oct 2025. No final fair use ruling in AI training expected until **summer 2026 at earliest**.

#### Privacy / Evidence Cases

| Case | Court | Key Ruling |
|------|-------|-----------|
| **US v. Heppner (Feb 2026)** | S.D.N.Y. | AI chats (Claude specifically) have NO attorney-client privilege. Discoverable. |
| **FBI CEO AI Chat Seizure (2026)** | Federal | Only narrow exception: if attorney specifically directed client to use AI for work product. |
| **Google Gemini Class Action (Oct 2025)** | N.D. Cal. | Alleges non-consensual Gemini activation across Gmail/Chat/Meet. |
| **Google $425.7M Verdict (Dec 2025)** | Federal | ~98M users misled about data collection opt-outs. |
| **Character.AI Teen Suicides** | Multiple | Settled Jan 2026 (Google + Character.AI). Multiple teen deaths linked to chatbots. |

### **Regulatory Actions**

#### GDPR Enforcement

| Target | DPA | Fine/Action | Date |
|--------|-----|-------------|------|
| **OpenAI** | Italy (Garante) | €15M fine | Dec 2024 |
| **Replika (Luka Inc.)** | Italy (Garante) | €5M fine | May 2025 |
| **DeepSeek** | Italy (Garante) | Banned (data processing limitation) | Jan 2025 |
| **DeepSeek** | South Korea (PIPC) | Banned from app stores | Feb 2025 |

#### FTC Actions

- **Sept 2025:** Formal inquiry into AI companion chatbots (7 companies ordered to provide data on impacts to children)
- **Aug 2025:** Final orders against Workado LLC (false AI accuracy claims); suit against Air AI Technologies
- **Dec 2025:** Reopened/set aside Rytr LLC consent order (signaling enforcement shift)

#### EU AI Act

- **Feb 2, 2025:** Prohibited practices (Article 5) enforceable
- **Aug 2, 2025:** Full enforcement of prohibited practices
- **Aug 2, 2026:** High-risk AI system requirements become enforceable (employment, credit, education, law enforcement AI)
- **Penalties:** Up to €35M or 7% of global turnover
- **No enforcement actions announced yet** but investigations reportedly underway

#### US State AI Laws (Effective Jan 1, 2026)

| State | Law | Key Provision |
|-------|-----|---------------|
| **California** | SB 53 (Transparency in Frontier AI Act) | Risk management disclosure for frontier models (revenue >$500M) |
| **California** | AB 2013 (GAI Training Data Transparency Act) | Training data transparency requirements |
| **California** | SB 942 (AI Transparency Act) | Delayed to Aug 2, 2026 |
| **Texas** | RAIGA (Responsible AI Governance Act) | Prohibits AI systems that incite harm, deepfakes, CSAM |
| **Colorado** | Comprehensive AI Act | Coming June 2026 |
| **Illinois** | Employer AI Disclosure | AI disclosure requirements for employers |

**Federal preemption uncertainty:** Trump's Dec 11, 2025 executive order seeks to preempt state AI laws; enforceability TBD.

### **National Security / Surveillance**

- **No public confirmation** of NSLs served to AI companies (these come with gag orders)
- **OpenAI-Pentagon deal (Feb-Mar 2026):** OpenAI providing AI for classified Pentagon networks. States three "red lines" (no mass domestic surveillance, no autonomous weapons), but EFF criticizes contractual language as "weasel words" that don't give OpenAI a free-standing right to prohibit lawful government use.
- **Anthropic blacklisted** by Trump administration for refusing Pentagon weapons/surveillance contract
- **FISA Section 702** due to expire April 2026 if not renewed — significant debate expected
- **OpenAI monitoring system** scans all ChatGPT conversations for harmful content, escalating to human reviewers who can report to law enforcement

---

## PART 4: PRIVACY SCORECARD & RECOMMENDATIONS

### **Privacy Risk Matrix (March 2026)**

| Provider | Model | US Jurisdiction | Data Retention | Training Opt-Out | Subpoena Risk | Court Precedent | Regulatory Risk | **Overall Risk** |
|----------|-------|----------------|----------------|-----------------|---------------|----------------|----------------|------------------|
| **OpenAI** | GPT-5.4 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | Indefinite (30d if history off) | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ✅ NYT + Heppner | 🔴 €15M GDPR fine, Pentagon deal | **🔴 HIGHEST** |
| **Anthropic** | Opus 4.6 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | 5yr if opted in; 30d if not | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ✅ US v. Heppner (Claude) | 🟡 $1.5B settlement | **🔴 HIGHEST** |
| **Google** | Gemini 3.1 | 🔴 Yes (CA) | 30d consumer; ZDR on Vertex | ✅ Available | 🔴 Critical | ⚠️ $425M verdict | 🟡 Class action pending | **🟡 HIGH** |
| **DeepSeek** | V3.1 (API) | 🔴 China | Unspecified; stored in PRC | ⚠️ Unclear | ⚠️ China gov't | ❌ None | 🔴 Banned IT/KR/TX; pending US federal | **🔴 HIGHEST** |
| **DeepSeek** | V3.1 (Self-Host) | ✅ Your choice | ✅ You control | ✅ N/A | 🟢 None | ❌ N/A | 🟢 None | **🟢 LOWEST** |
| **Llama 4** | Self-Host | ✅ Your choice | ✅ You control | ✅ N/A | 🟢 None | ❌ N/A | 🟢 None | **🟢 LOWEST** |
| **Mistral 3** | Self-Host | ✅ Your choice | ✅ You control | ✅ N/A | 🟢 None | ❌ N/A | 🟢 None | **🟢 LOWEST** |

---

### **RECOMMENDATIONS FOR COMMON CLOUD DEPLOYMENT**

#### **For MVP (Months 1-6): Use API with Maximum Privacy Settings**

**Option 1: Claude Opus 4.6 API (Recommended)**
- ✅ Commercial Terms / Data Processing Agreement (no training, Anthropic acts as processor)
- ✅ Best coding performance (agent + complex reasoning leader)
- ✅ Extended thinking + adaptive thinking
- ✅ Refused Pentagon weapons/surveillance contract (values alignment)
- ❌ US jurisdiction (NSL risk)
- ❌ 5-year retention if using consumer account with training enabled

**Setup:**
```
1. Sign up for Claude API (Commercial Terms, NOT consumer)
2. Use API keys only (never Claude.ai consumer interface)
3. Rotate API keys monthly (minimize exposure window)
4. Log all prompts locally (your infrastructure, encrypted)
5. Never include real names, immigration status, or medical info in prompts
```

**Cost:** $5/M input, $25/M output (Opus 4.6); $3/M input, $15/M output (Sonnet 4.6)

---

**Option 2: Self-Hosted Open-Source Models (Maximum Privacy)**
- ✅ You control all data (Iceland/Switzerland servers)
- ✅ Zero external transmission
- ✅ No company can be subpoenaed
- ✅ Multiple strong options: Llama 4 Maverick, DeepSeek V3.1, Mistral Large 3
- ❌ Requires significant compute (GPUs)
- ❌ Complex setup

**Best self-hosted options (March 2026):**

| Model | License | Why |
|-------|---------|-----|
| Llama 4 Scout (17B active) | Open-weight | Fits single H100; 10M context; strong multimodal |
| Llama 4 Maverick (17B active) | Open-weight | Beats GPT-4o on benchmarks |
| DeepSeek V3.1 (671B MoE) | MIT | Best open-source general model; requires serious GPU cluster |
| Mistral Large 3 (41B/675B MoE) | Apache 2.0 | Strong reasoning; 256K context; fully open-source |

**Cost:** €500-1000/mo GPU server (8x A100 equivalent for large models); much less for Llama 4 Scout on single H100

---

**Option 3: Gemini 3.1 Pro via Vertex AI (EU-Compliant Compromise)**
- ✅ Native EU data residency (configurable per project)
- ✅ Per-call Zero Data Retention
- ✅ GDPR-compliant infrastructure with DPA
- ✅ SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA
- ⚠️ Still US parent company (Google)
- ⚠️ ZDR requires explicit per-call configuration (not automatic)
- ⚠️ Class action pending over non-consensual Gemini activation

**Setup:**
```
1. Sign up for Google Cloud (Vertex AI)
2. Select EU region (europe-west1 = Belgium, europe-west3 = Frankfurt)
3. Enable Zero Data Retention per API call
4. Disable Session Resumption for true ZDR
5. Configure data residency = EU only
6. Use Vertex AI API (NOT Gemini consumer app)
```

**Cost:** $2/M input, $12/M output (Gemini 3.1 Pro); $0.50/$3.00 (Gemini 3 Flash)

---

### **CRITICAL: NEVER Use Consumer Interfaces for Deployment**

**❌ DO NOT USE:**
- ChatGPT.com (consumer interface — indefinite retention, monitoring system, litigation hold)
- Claude.ai (consumer interface — 5-year retention if training enabled)
- Gemini app (consumer interface — class action over non-consensual features)

**✅ ALWAYS USE:**
- Claude API (Commercial Terms / DPA)
- OpenAI API (with ZDR agreement if qualifying)
- Vertex AI (Gemini API with per-call ZDR)

## PART 6: FINAL RECOMMENDATIONS

### **For Common Cloud Deployment (Vulnerable Communities)**

**Phase 1 (MVP, Months 1-6):**
- ✅ **Use: Claude Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6 API (Commercial Terms)**
  - Best coding + reasoning performance
  - Commercial Terms = Anthropic acts as data processor, no training
  - Anthropic refused Pentagon weapons/surveillance contract (values alignment)
  - US jurisdiction (NSL risk) but acceptable for MVP
  - Cost: ~$5-25/M tokens (Opus), ~$3-15/M (Sonnet)

- ⚠️ **Avoid: Consumer interfaces** (ChatGPT.com, Claude.ai, Gemini app)
  - Indefinite or 5-year retention
  - Litigation holds override user settings
  - No attorney-client privilege (US v. Heppner)

- ⚠️ **Avoid: DeepSeek API**
  - China jurisdiction, data stored in PRC
  - Banned in multiple countries
  - Third-party analysis found data sharing with China Mobile

- ⚠️ **Avoid: OpenAI for sensitive work**
  - Pentagon contract creates government entanglement
  - Monitoring system scans all conversations
  - Litigation hold preserves all consumer data

---

**Phase 2 (Scale, Months 6-12):**
- ✅ **Add: Gemini 3.1 Pro (Vertex AI, EU region)**
  - Native EU data residency
  - Per-call Zero Data Retention
  - Best GDPR posture of major US providers

- ✅ **Begin: Self-hosted pilot** (Llama 4 Scout on single H100)
  - Test feasibility with smallest capable model
  - Evaluate latency and quality vs API models

---

**Phase 3 (Mature, Year 2+):**
- ✅ **Migrate to: Self-hosted open-source models**
  - **Llama 4 Maverick/Behemoth** (open-weight, natively multimodal)
  - **DeepSeek V3.1** or V4 when released (MIT License)
  - **Mistral Large 3** (Apache 2.0, 256K context)
  - Deploy on Iceland/Switzerland servers
  - Zero external API calls = maximum privacy

---

## CONCLUSION

**March 2026 Reality:**
- ALL major AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) are US-based, subject to NSLs, FISA courts, and subpoenas
- **AI conversations have no legal privilege** (US v. Heppner, Feb 2026) — explicitly ruled for Claude conversations
- **20 million ChatGPT logs** ordered produced in NYT case (Jan 2026) — your data may be included
- **OpenAI scans all conversations** and can escalate to law enforcement
- **OpenAI now contracts with the Pentagon** for classified AI networks
- **DeepSeek data confirmed stored in China**, shared with state-owned entities, banned in multiple jurisdictions
- **GDPR enforcement is real:** €15M fine (OpenAI), €5M fine (Replika), DeepSeek banned in Italy
- **$3.5B+ in AI governance fines and settlements** paid by Big Tech in 2025 alone

**Best Practice for Vulnerable Communities:**
1. **Use API with Commercial Terms / DPA** (not consumer interfaces)
2. **Rotate API keys monthly** (minimize exposure window)
3. **Log all prompts locally** (your encrypted infrastructure)
4. **Plan migration to self-hosted** (Year 2+, Iceland/Switzerland)
5. **Never use AI for:**
   - Real names of vulnerable individuals
   - Immigration status information
   - Legal strategy discussions (**no attorney-client privilege** — confirmed by federal court)
   - Medical records (HIPAA violations)
   - Financial data (PCI-DSS violations)
6. **Prefer Anthropic over OpenAI** for values alignment (refused Pentagon weapons contract; OpenAI accepted)
7. **Monitor FISA Section 702 renewal** (expires April 2026 — could expand or limit surveillance powers)

**Nicholas Merrill's Legacy:** "You can't encrypt your way out of bad infrastructure. You have to design privacy into the infrastructure itself."

**For Common Cloud:** Start with Claude Opus 4.6 API (Commercial Terms) for speed and values alignment; add Vertex AI Gemini for EU residency; plan migration to self-hosted Llama 4 / Mistral 3 / DeepSeek on Iceland servers for maximum sovereignty.

    20→- **Privacy policy updates:** Anthropic (Jan 2026), OpenAI DPA (Jan 2026); Anthropic 5-year retention still in effect for opted-in consumers

    23→### Current Model Lineup (March 2026)

    36→| Mistral | **Mistral Large 3 / Ministral 3** | Dec 2025 |

    39→
    40→## PART 1: MARCH 2026 MODEL LANDSCAPE
    41→
    42→### **OpenAI GPT-5.4 / GPT-5.2 / o3-pro**
    43→
    44→**Release Dates:**

    46→- GPT-5.2: December 2025
    47→- GPT-5.1: November 2025
    48→- o3-pro / o4-mini: 2025 (reasoning models)
    49→

    51→- **1M context window**
    52→- **Native computer-use:** First OpenAI model with built-in computer interaction
    53→- **Pro tier:** Higher-quality output at premium pricing
    54→- **GPT-5-mini and GPT-5-nano** available for budget deployments
    55→
    56→**API Pricing (per 1M tokens):**
    57→
    58→| Model | Input | Output |
    59→|-------|-------|--------|
    60→| GPT-5.4 | $2.50 | $15.00 |

    75→
    76→### **Claude Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6 / Haiku 4.5**
    77→
    78→**Release Dates:**
    79→- Opus 4.6: February 5, 2026
    80→- Sonnet 4.6: February 17, 2026
    81→- Haiku 4.5: October 1, 2025 (no Haiku 4.6 yet)

    83→
    84→**Key Features:**
    85→- **Opus 4.6:** Most intelligent model; best for agents and complex coding. 200K context (1M beta). Extended thinking + adaptive thinking.
    86→- **Sonnet 4.6:** Everyday workhorse; reportedly outperforms Opus on some real-world office tasks. 200K context (1M beta).

    90→**API Pricing (per 1M tokens):**

    92→| Model | Input | Output |
    93→|-------|-------|--------|
    94→| Claude Opus 4.6 | $5.00 | $25.00 |
    95→| Claude Sonnet 4.6 | $3.00 | $15.00 |
    96→| Claude Haiku 4.5 | $1.00 | $5.00 |
    97→
    98→**Availability:**
    99→- Claude.ai (Pro, Max, Team Premium users)
   100→- Claude Code (Pro users)
   101→- API (anthropic.com)
   102→- Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI
   103→
   104→---
   105→
   106→### **Google Gemini 3.1 Pro / Gemini 3 Flash**
   107→
   108→**Release Dates:**
   109→- Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview: February 2026
   110→- Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview: February 2026
   111→- Gemini 3 Flash Preview: January 2026
   112→- Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash: Stable (2025)
   113→
   114→**Key Features (Gemini 3.1 Pro):**
   115→- **77.1% ARC-AGI-2 score**
   116→- **Native video understanding**
   117→- **Agentic capabilities**
   118→- **1M context window**
   119→
   120→**API Pricing (per 1M tokens):**
   121→
   122→| Model | Input | Output |
   123→|-------|-------|--------|
   124→| Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview | $2.00 (≤200K) / $4.00 | $12.00 / $18.00 |
   125→| Gemini 3 Flash Preview | $0.50 | $3.00 |
   126→| Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite | $0.25 | $1.50 |
   127→| Gemini 2.5 Pro | $1.25 / $2.50 | $10.00 / $15.00 |
   128→| Gemini 2.5 Flash | $0.30 | $2.50 |
   129→
   130→**Consumer Plans:**
   131→- Free tier available through Google AI Studio
   132→- Pro: $19.99/mo
   133→- Ultra: $124.99/3 months
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→### **DeepSeek V3.1 / V3 / R1**
   138→
   139→**Status (March 2026):**
   140→- **DeepSeek V3.1:** Released August 21, 2025. MIT License. Hybrid thinking/non-thinking modes. +40% over V3 on SWE-bench.
   141→- **DeepSeek V3 (V3-0324):** 671B MoE, still available.
   142→- **DeepSeek R1:** Dedicated reasoning model (OpenAI o1-level).
   143→- **DeepSeek V4:** NOT YET RELEASED. Originally targeted mid-Feb 2026, delayed. Expected ~1 trillion params.
   144→- **DeepSeek R2:** NOT YET RELEASED. Delayed due to data labeling and chip constraints.
   145→
   146→**API Pricing:** ~$0.55/M input tokens (dramatically cheaper than Western competitors).
   147→
   148→**Self-Hosted:** Open-source weights available on HuggingFace.
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→### **Meta Llama 4 (Open-Weight)**
   153→
   154→**Released:** Spring 2025
   155→
   156→| Model | Active Params | Experts | Key Feature |
   157→|-------|--------------|---------|-------------|
   158→| Llama 4 Scout | 17B | 16 (MoE) | 10M context window. Fits single H100. |
   159→| Llama 4 Maverick | 17B | 128 (MoE) | Beats GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 Flash on benchmarks. |
   160→| Llama 4 Behemoth | 288B | 16 (MoE) | Outperforms GPT-4.5, Claude Sonnet 3.7, Gemini 2.0 Pro on STEM. |
   161→
   162→- Natively multimodal (not bolted-on vision)
   163→- Free and open-weight
   164→- Mixture-of-Experts architecture
   165→
   166→---
   167→
   168→### **Mistral 3 (Open-Source, Apache 2.0)**
   169→
   170→**Released:** December 2025
   171→
   172→| Model | Parameters | Notes |
   173→|-------|-----------|-------|
   174→| Mistral Large 3 | 41B active / 675B MoE | Flagship. 256K context. Apache 2.0. |
   175→| Ministral 3 14B | 14B dense | Base, instruct, reasoning variants. |
   176→| Ministral 3 8B | 8B dense | Same variant options. |
   177→| Ministral 3 3B | 3B dense | Edge-friendly. |
   178→| Devstral 2 / Small 2 | 24B | Coding-focused. |
   179→
   180→---
   181→
   182→## PART 2: PRIVACY & THREAT ANALYSIS BY PROVIDER
   183→
   184→### **CRITICAL FINDING: AI CHAT HISTORY HAS NO LEGAL PRIVILEGE**
   185→
   186→**Key Court Rulings (2025-2026):**
   187→
   188→1. **US v. Heppner (S.D.N.Y., Feb 10, 2026):** Judge Jed Rakoff ruled that conversations between a criminal defendant and Anthropic's Claude are **NOT protected** by attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine. Reasoning: Claude is not a lawyer, and "discussion of legal issues between two non-attorneys is not protected." The defendant had no reasonable expectation of confidentiality. This is the first federal ruling directly addressing AI chat privilege.
   189→
   190→2. **NYT v. OpenAI (S.D.N.Y., Jan 2026):** Judge Sidney Stein upheld an order compelling OpenAI to produce its **entire 20-million-log sample** of ChatGPT conversations. OpenAI's attempt to hand over only cherry-picked logs was rejected. Only Enterprise, Education, and ZDR customers are excluded from the preservation order.
   191→
   192→3. **FBI Seizure of CEO's AI Chats (2026):** FBI seized a CEO's computer and sought access to his AI chat history. Court left only a narrow, untested exception: if an attorney specifically directed the client to conduct AI research as part of attorney work product.
   193→
   194→4. **Criminal Prosecutions (2025):** Three criminal cases cited ChatGPT conversations as evidence — involving child exploitation, arson, and vandalism.
   195→
   196→5. **Divorce/Custody Cases (2024-2026):** Multiple cases used ChatGPT/Claude history as evidence. California courts are now implementing AI document review for family law (mandatory by July 2025 for courts serving 500K+ populations).
   197→
   198→**Legal Precedent Established:**
   199→- AI chat history is treated like any digital evidence (texts, emails)
   200→- Admissible in court
   201→- **No attorney-client privilege** (US v. Heppner)
   202→- Deletion after preservation notice = spoliation (sanctions)
   203→- Half of AI users are unaware their chats can be compelled as evidence (survey, 2025)
   204→- Sam Altman publicly warned that people treating ChatGPT like a therapist/lawyer should understand those conversations can be subpoenaed
   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→### **OpenAI Transparency: Government Requests (H1 2025)**
   209→
   210→OpenAI's published government data request report for January-June 2025:
   211→- **119 requests** for user account information
   212→- **26 requests** for chat content
   213→- **1 emergency request**
   214→- Policy: requires subpoena for account info, valid warrant for content
   215→
   216→**OpenAI also operates a monitoring system** that scans all ChatGPT conversations for potentially harmful content, escalating concerning conversations to human reviewers who can report to law enforcement.
   217→
   218→---
   219→
   220→### **OpenAI (ChatGPT / GPT-5.4) Privacy Analysis**
   221→
   222→#### **Data Retention Policy (Updated Jan 2026)**
   223→
   224→**ChatGPT Consumer (Plus/Pro, $20-200/mo):**
   225→- **Default:** Chats retained indefinitely for model improvement unless chat history is disabled
   226→- **History off:** Retained 30 days for abuse monitoring, then deleted; not used for training
   227→- **BUT:** NYT litigation hold requires preservation regardless of user settings. Only Enterprise, Education, and ZDR customers are excluded.
   228→
   229→**ChatGPT API / Business:**
   230→- **Default API:** 30-day retention for safety monitoring, then auto-deleted
   231→- **API data not used for training by default** (since March 2023)
   232→- **Zero Data Retention (ZDR):** Available for qualifying enterprise customers — no storage of prompts or responses, no human review. Requires prior OpenAI approval. Not all endpoints qualify.
   233→- **Enterprise/Team:** Data never used for training
   234→
   235→**Government Data Request Policy (effective Jan 1, 2026):** New published policy formalizing law enforcement request procedures.
   236→
   237→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   238→
   239→**How to Opt Out:**
   240→1. Settings → Data Controls → "Improve the model for everyone" (toggle off)
   241→2. OR: Enable "Temporary Chat" mode
   242→3. API: Data not used for training by default
   243→
   244→**Effect:**
   245→- ✅ Your chats won't train future models
   246→- ⚠️ Still retained 30 days (abuse monitoring)
   247→- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides — NYT litigation hold)
   248→
   249→---
   250→
   251→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   252→
   253→| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
   254→|----------|-----------|-----|
   255→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | FBI can serve NSL directly to OpenAI; gag order prevents disclosure; monitoring system already scans all chats |
   256→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Texas/AZ AG can subpoena OpenAI (US company, US jurisdiction); immediate compliance likely |
   257→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | OpenAI is US-based; fined €15M by Italy (Dec 2024); cross-border transfer concerns |
   258→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Multiple precedents; courts routinely subpoena ChatGPT history |
   259→| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer can request logs if using company API keys; individual use can be subpoenaed |
   260→| **Pentagon/Military** | 🔴 **NEW RISK** | OpenAI contracted for classified Pentagon networks (Feb-Mar 2026); contractual "red lines" on mass surveillance criticized as insufficient by EFF |
   261→
   262→**Key Risk:** OpenAI is **US-based (San Francisco)**, subject to US courts, Five Eyes intelligence sharing, and NSL/FISA court orders. New Pentagon contract creates additional government entanglement.
   263→
   264→---
   265→
   266→### **Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6) Privacy Analysis**
   267→
   268→#### **Data Retention Policy (Updated Jan 12, 2026)**
   269→
   270→**MAJOR POLICY (Sept 2025, still in effect):**
   271→- **Previous:** 30-day retention (all users)
   272→- **Current:** **5-year retention** of de-identified data if you opt in to model training
   273→
   274→**Consumer Accounts (Free, Pro, Max):**
   275→
   276→| Setting | Retention | Training Use |
   277→|---------|-----------|-------------|
   278→| "Help improve Claude" = **ON** | 5 years (de-identified) | Yes |
   279→| "Help improve Claude" = **OFF** | 30 days then deleted | No |
   280→| Trust & safety flagged content | Up to 2 years (inputs/outputs) | N/A |
   281→| Trust & safety classification scores | Up to 7 years | N/A |
   282→| User feedback submissions | 5 years | N/A |
   283→
   284→**Key clarification:** 5-year retention only covers **new or resumed chats** after the setting is enabled — not retroactive conversations.
   285→
   286→**Business/Enterprise (Claude for Work, API, Bedrock, Vertex):**
   287→- Anthropic acts as **data processor** under separate Data Processing Agreements
   288→- Data is **NOT used for training**
   289→- Consumer privacy policy does not apply
   290→- Includes: Claude for Work, Claude for Education, Claude Gov
   291→
   292→**Jan 2026 Update:** Added Consumer Health Data Privacy Policy for US users integrating third-party health apps; consolidated regional supplemental disclosures.
   293→
   294→#### **GDPR Entity:**
   295→- **EEA/UK/Switzerland:** Anthropic Ireland, Limited
   296→- **All other regions:** Anthropic PBC (San Francisco, CA)
   297→- Cross-border transfers via adequacy decisions and Standard Contractual Clauses
   298→
   299→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   300→
   301→**How to Opt Out:**
   302→1. Settings → Privacy Settings → "Help improve Claude" (toggle OFF)
   303→2. File Data Processing Objection (optional, for formal record)
   304→
   305→**Effect:**
   306→- ✅ Data not used for training
   307→- ✅ Retention reduced to 30 days
   308→- ❌ Lose personalization/memory features
   309→- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)
   310→- ❌ Trust & safety exceptions still apply (2-7 year retention for flagged content)
   311→
   312→#### **Anthropic Legal Exposure (New Since Dec 2025)**
   313→
   314→- **Bartz v. Anthropic ($1.5B settlement, Aug 2025):** Anthropic settled a class action over using pirated books (from shadow libraries) to train Claude. ~$3,000 per title for ~500,000 books. Claims deadline: March 30, 2026. Final approval hearing: April 2026.
   315→- **Reddit v. Anthropic (Feb 2026):** Reddit sued Anthropic for deploying ~24,000 automated bots to scrape Reddit content over 100,000 times. Claims breach of contract, unjust enrichment, trespass to chattels.
   316→- **Music Publishers v. Anthropic (M.D. Tenn., ongoing):** UMG, Concord, and others sued for massive-scale music lyric copyright infringement via web scraping.
   317→- **Anthropic refused Pentagon contract (Feb 2026):** Blacklisted by Trump administration for refusing to allow AI use for autonomous weapons or mass domestic surveillance. OpenAI took the contract instead.
   318→
   319→---
   320→
   321→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   322→
   323→| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
   324→|----------|-----------|-----|
   325→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Anthropic is US-based (SF); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; 5-year retention if opted in = extended exposure |
   326→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction, immediate compliance likely |
   327→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **HIGH** | US-based company with Irish subsidiary; GDPR deletion rights may conflict with 5-year retention |
   328→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US v. Heppner (Feb 2026) explicitly addressed Claude conversations — no privilege |
   329→| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Employer API keys = employer can request logs; individual use can be subpoenaed |
   330→
   331→**Key Risk:** Anthropic is **US-based (San Francisco)**, subject to same legal framework as OpenAI. US v. Heppner specifically established that Claude conversations are discoverable.
   332→
   333→---
   334→
   335→### **Google (Gemini 3.1 Pro) Privacy Analysis**
   336→
   337→#### **Data Retention Policy (March 2026)**
   338→
   339→**Gemini Consumer App:**
   340→- **Default:** Prompts and responses stored up to 30 days for debugging and abuse detection
   341→- **Opt-out:** Settings → Gemini Apps Activity → toggle OFF
   342→- **If disabled:** Conversations not saved, not used for training; metadata still logged
   343→
   344→**Vertex AI (Business):**
   345→- **In-memory cache:** 24-hour TTL, project-level isolation
   346→- **Zero Data Retention:** Must be explicitly enabled **per API call** by setting a specific field in the request
   347→- **Session Resumption must NOT be enabled** to achieve true ZDR
   348→- SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA available
   349→
   350→**Google Workspace:**
   351→- Google states it will **not use customer data to train or fine-tune AI/ML models** without prior permission
   352→- Admins can shorten or disable prompt storage entirely
   353→
   354→**EU Data Residency:**
   355→- Configurable data regions: **US**, **EU**, or **No preference**
   356→- Vertex AI Platform supports configurable data location
   357→- Supported EU regions: europe-west1 (Belgium), europe-west3 (Frankfurt), etc.
   358→
   359→**Class Action (Oct 2025):** Lawsuit alleges Google activated Gemini AI features across Gmail, Chat, and Meet **without user consent**, enabling AI to read private communications. Putative class of all U.S. Google account holders.
   360→
   361→**$425.7M Verdict (Dec 2025):** Federal jury awarded $425.7M to ~98M Google users misled about data collection opt-outs on mobile devices.
   362→
   363→#### **Training Data Opt-Out**
   364→
   365→**Gemini App:**
   366→1. Gemini Apps Activity settings → Toggle OFF "Save Gemini Apps Activity"
   367→2. Delete existing history (optional)
   368→
   369→**Workspace:** Admin console → Settings → Disable AI training
   370→
   371→**Effect:**
   372→- ✅ Conversations not used for training
   373→- ⚠️ Metadata still logged (abuse/compliance)
   374→- ❌ Can still be subpoenaed (court overrides)
   375→
   376→---
   377→
   378→#### **Subpoena Risk Analysis**
   379→
   380→| Scenario | Risk Level | Why |
   381→|----------|-----------|-----|
   382→| **NSL (Scenario 1)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Google is US-based (CA); subject to NSLs, FISA courts; extensive data infrastructure |
   383→| **State Subpoena (Scenario 3)** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | US jurisdiction; Google has cooperated with law enforcement historically |
   384→| **GDPR Request (Scenario 2)** | 🟡 **MODERATE** | Google has EU data centers and configurable data residency; better than OpenAI/Anthropic but still US parent company |
   385→| **Divorce/Custody** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Same risk as ChatGPT/Claude |
   386→| **Workplace Litigation** | 🔴 **CRITICAL** | Workspace admins can access user logs; class action alleging non-consensual Gemini activation pending |
   387→
   388→**Advantage over OpenAI/Anthropic:** Google has native EU data residency options, configurable per Vertex AI project. Best GDPR posture of the three major US providers.
   389→
   390→---
   391→
   392→### **DeepSeek (V3.1) Privacy Analysis**
   393→
   394→#### **Data Retention Policy (March 2026)**
   395→
   396→**DeepSeek API:**
   397→- **China-based:** Hangzhou DeepSeek Artificial Intelligence Co., Ltd.
   398→- **All data stored in the People's Republic of China** (explicitly stated in privacy policy)
   399→- **No specific retention periods disclosed:** Policy says "as long as necessary to provide our Services" — vague and non-compliant with GDPR transparency requirements
   400→- **Data collected:** Account info, user inputs (text, voice, prompts, uploaded files, chat history), device/network info (IP, device model, OS, device ID), approximate location, cookies, payment data
   401→- **Training use:** Personal data used "to improve and develop the Services and to train and improve our technology" — opt-out mechanism unclear
   402→- **Third-party analysis found:** DeepSeek app can capture login information and share it with China Mobile (state-owned entity)
   403→- **GDPR representative:** Prighter Group appointed as Art. 27 representative, but DeepSeek has claimed GDPR does not apply to it
   404→
   405→**Self-Hosted (Open-Source):**
   406→- You control all data — run on your own servers
   407→- No external transmission

   538→#### **For MVP (Months 1-6): Use API with Maximum Privacy Settings**
   539→
   540→**Option 1: Claude Opus 4.6 API (Recommended)**
   541→- ✅ Commercial Terms / Data Processing Agreement (no training, Anthropic acts as processor)
   542→- ✅ Best coding performance (agent + complex reasoning leader)
   543→- ✅ Extended thinking + adaptive thinking
   544→- ✅ Refused Pentagon weapons/surveillance contract (values alignment)
   545→- ❌ US jurisdiction (NSL risk)
   546→- ❌ 5-year retention if using consumer account with training enabled
   547→
   548→**Setup:**
   549→```
   550→1. Sign up for Claude API (Commercial Terms, NOT consumer)
   551→2. Use API keys only (never Claude.ai consumer interface)
   552→3. Rotate API keys monthly (minimize exposure window)
   553→4. Log all prompts locally (your infrastructure, encrypted)
   554→5. Never include real names, immigration status, or medical info in prompts
   555→```

   689→## CONCLUSION
   690→
   691→**March 2026 Reality:**
   692→- ALL major AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) are US-based, subject to NSLs, FISA courts, and subpoenas
   693→- **AI conversations have no legal privilege** (US v. Heppner, Feb 2026) — explicitly ruled for Claude conversations
   694→- **20 million ChatGPT logs** ordered produced in NYT case (Jan 2026) — your data may be included
   695→- **OpenAI scans all conversations** and can escalate to law enforcement
   696→- **OpenAI now contracts with the Pentagon** for classified AI networks
   697→- **DeepSeek data confirmed stored in China**, shared with state-owned entities, banned in multiple jurisdictions
   698→- **GDPR enforcement is real:** €15M fine (OpenAI), €5M fine (Replika), DeepSeek banned in Italy
   699→- **$3.5B+ in AI governance fines and settlements** paid by Big Tech in 2025 alone
   700→
   701→**Best Practice for Vulnerable Communities:**
   702→1. **Use API with Commercial Terms / DPA** (not consumer interfaces)
   703→2. **Rotate API keys monthly** (minimize exposure window)
   704→3. **Log all prompts locally** (your encrypted infrastructure)
   705→4. **Plan migration to self-hosted** (Year 2+, Iceland/Switzerland)
   706→5. **Never use AI for:**
   707→   - Real names of vulnerable individuals
   708→   - Immigration status information
   709→   - Legal strategy discussions (**no attorney-client privilege** — confirmed by federal court)
   710→   - Medical records (HIPAA violations)
   711→   - Financial data (PCI-DSS violations)
   712→6. **Prefer Anthropic over OpenAI** for values alignment (refused Pentagon weapons contract; OpenAI accepted)
   713→7. **Monitor FISA Section 702 renewal** (expires April 2026 — could expand or limit surveillance powers)
   714→
   715→**Nicholas Merrill's Legacy:** "You can't encrypt your way out of bad infrastructure. You have to design privacy into the infrastructure itself."
   716→
   717→**For Common Cloud:** Start with Claude Opus 4.6 API (Commercial Terms) for speed and values alignment; add Vertex AI Gemini for EU residency; plan migration to self-hosted Llama 4 / Mistral 3 / DeepSeek on Iceland servers for maximum sovereignty.
